title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
abstract: Introduces RAG, combining a parametric seq2seq model with a non-parametric dense retriever over Wikipedia to ground generation in retrieved passages, improving factuality on knowledge-intensive tasks.

title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
abstract: Proposes a reference-free framework to evaluate RAG pipelines along faithfulness, answer relevance, and context relevance, enabling fast automated iteration without ground-truth answers.

title: FActScore: Fine-grained Atomic Evaluation of Factual Precision in Long Form Text Generation
abstract: Decomposes generated text into atomic facts and scores the percentage supported by a reliable knowledge source, giving fine-grained factual-precision measurement for long-form generation.

title: SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection for Generative Large Language Models
abstract: Detects hallucinations without external resources by sampling multiple responses and measuring consistency: factual statements are stable across samples while hallucinations diverge.

title: ARES: An Automated Evaluation Framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
abstract: Trains lightweight LLM judges with synthetic data plus prediction-powered inference to score RAG systems on context relevance, answer faithfulness, and answer relevance with statistical confidence.

title: Chain-of-Verification Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Models
abstract: CoVe drafts an answer, plans verification questions, answers them independently to avoid copying the draft's errors, then revises — reducing factual hallucinations across list and long-form tasks.

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
abstract: Trains a model to adaptively retrieve and to emit reflection tokens that critique its own retrieval and generation for relevance and support, improving factuality and citation accuracy.

title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
abstract: CRAG adds a lightweight retrieval evaluator that grades retrieved documents and triggers corrective actions (web search, knowledge refinement) when retrieval is wrong, improving robustness.

title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
abstract: Shows model performance degrades when relevant information is in the middle of a long context, with a U-shaped position bias — important for ordering retrieved passages in RAG.

title: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering
abstract: Learns dense passage embeddings with a dual-encoder trained on question-passage pairs, outperforming BM25 for open-domain QA retrieval.

title: Survey of Hallucination in Natural Language Generation
abstract: Comprehensive survey of hallucination definitions, causes, metrics, and mitigation methods across summarization, dialogue, QA, and data-to-text generation.

title: TruthfulQA: Measuring How Models Mimic Human Falsehoods
abstract: A benchmark of questions where humans answer falsely due to misconceptions; larger models can be less truthful, motivating truthfulness-specific evaluation.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
abstract: Interleaves chain-of-thought reasoning with tool actions so models can plan, call tools, and incorporate observations — the basis for many tool-using agents.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
abstract: Self-supervised method teaching LLMs when and how to call external APIs (calculator, search, QA) by inserting and filtering API calls that reduce loss.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Agents reflect verbally on task feedback, storing self-critiques in episodic memory to improve subsequent attempts without weight updates — strong gains on decision-making and coding.

title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
abstract: Trains a harmless assistant using AI-generated critiques and revisions against a set of principles (a constitution), reducing reliance on human harmfulness labels.

title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
abstract: Freezes pretrained weights and injects trainable low-rank decomposition matrices into each layer, cutting trainable parameters by orders of magnitude with no inference latency.

title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
abstract: Fine-tunes a 4-bit NF4-quantized frozen base model through LoRA adapters with paged optimizers, enabling 65B fine-tuning on a single 48GB GPU with near-full-precision quality.

title: DoRA: Weight-Decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation
abstract: Decomposes pretrained weights into magnitude and direction and applies LoRA to the direction, closing much of the accuracy gap to full fine-tuning while keeping LoRA's efficiency.

title: Scaling Down to Scale Up: A Guide to Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
abstract: Survey and taxonomy of parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods (additive, selective, reparameterization) with practical comparisons of efficiency, memory, and quality trade-offs.

title: LoRA Learns Less and Forgets Less
abstract: Empirical study showing LoRA underperforms full fine-tuning on large domain shifts but better preserves base-model capabilities (forgets less), with guidance on rank and target modules.

title: The Power of Scale for Parameter-Efficient Prompt Tuning
abstract: Learns soft prompts (continuous task embeddings) while freezing the model; competitiveness with full fine-tuning grows with model scale.

title: Why do tree-based models still outperform deep learning on typical tabular data?
abstract: Benchmarks tree ensembles vs deep models on tabular data, finding trees still win on medium-sized datasets due to inductive biases around irregular functions and uninformative features.

title: TabLLM: Few-shot Classification of Tabular Data with Large Language Models
abstract: Serializes tabular rows to natural language and fine-tunes an LLM, achieving strong few-shot tabular classification that can beat tree baselines in very low-data regimes.
